title: OSP Profile: IT Managed Services v1
description: Vocabulary for managed IT services: hosting, databases, Kubernetes, security, backup, monitoring, and SRE-on-demand. Captures the operational parameters — SLA tiers, support coverage, recovery objectives, compliance frameworks — that drive provider selection beyond generic cloud features.

service_categories.description: Service lines the provider actively operates. Distinct from cloud services resold without operational responsibility.

cloud_providers.description: Hyperscaler and platform targets supported. 'swiss_sovereign' covers CH-located sovereign clouds, relevant where data residency is regulated.

os_support.description: Operating systems the provider manages (monitoring, patching, support).

managed_databases.description: Database engines the provider operates. Managing a database is materially different from running a VM that hosts one.

iac_tools_supported.description: Infrastructure-as-code tools the provider uses and can maintain alongside customer IaC.

uptime_sla_tiers.description: Available uptime SLA tiers, expressed as fractions (0.99 = 99%, 0.9995 = 99.95%). Providers typically offer multiple.

incident_response_times.description: Time-to-acknowledge by incident severity, in minutes. Severity 1 is customer-down / data-loss.

backup_retention_max_days.description: Maximum retention horizon for managed backups, in days. Regulated industries often require ≥ 2555 (7 years).

dr_rto_minutes.description: Best available Recovery Time Objective (RTO), in minutes. Time from incident declaration to service restoration.

dr_rpo_minutes.description: Best available Recovery Point Objective (RPO), in minutes. Maximum acceptable data loss window.

security_certifications.description: Third-party attestations held by the provider organisation (not merely customer-inherited from the hyperscaler).

compliance_frameworks.description: Regulatory and best-practice frameworks the managed environments are engineered against.

patch_management.description: How patches are applied and on what cadence.

data_residency_guaranteed.description: Whether the provider contractually guarantees that customer data does not leave declared jurisdictions, including backups and operational access.

single_tenant_available.description: Whether single-tenant deployment is available. Relevant for regulated and high-security workloads.

agent_ops_enabled.description: Whether the provider exposes an agent-operable control plane (MCP, API, structured events) for autonomous management by external AI agents.
